_readme: Azure Data Factory Pipeline: Azure SQL → Snowflake
HOW TO IMPORT THIS INTO ADF:
1. portal.azure.com → Create a resource → Data Factory
2. Open your Data Factory → Author (pencil icon)
3. Click + → Pipeline → Import from template OR
create manually using the steps below.
MANUAL SETUP (takes ~30 mins, follow in order):
A) Create Linked Services (Manage tab → Linked Services):
- AzureSqlDatabase: connect to your Azure SQL DB
- Snowflake: connect to your Snowflake account
C) Create Pipeline with Copy Activity (described below)
This file documents the pipeline configuration for your README.

pipeline.name: PL_AzureSQL_To_Snowflake_Hospitality
pipeline.description: Daily pipeline copying hospitality transactions from Azure SQL to Snowflake for analytics

activities.name: Copy_Transactions_To_Snowflake
activities.description: Copies all transaction records from Azure SQL dbo.transactions into Snowflake RAW.TRANSACTIONS

source.sqlReaderQuery: SELECT * FROM dbo.transactions WHERE loaded_at >= DATEADD(hour, -25, GETUTCDATE())
source.description: Incremental load — last 25 hours to handle daily overlap

activities.description_staging: ADF uses Azure Blob Storage as a staging area when copying to Snowflake

activities.name: Log_Pipeline_Run
activities.description: Writes pipeline run metadata to Azure SQL audit table after copy completes
